Game Development · Game Design · Music Production

Pisupati
Rama Sridhrut

I build fun, memorable, and deeply enjoyable games. Currently an engineering student, I pair technical systems work with narrative-driven design to craft interactive experiences that stick.

Engines

Scroll

About me

A developer who likes to do every part of the game

Who I am

I'm a 4th-year Electrical & Electronics Engineering student at MIT Manipal, and a game developer who genuinely loves the craft. I'm drawn to games with strong narrative and a feel that just clicks in your hands.

I work across all the aspects of a game: engineering the systems, designing the mechanics, writing the story, and scoring the audio. Most of my work lives in Godot, with growing time in Unreal and Unity.

I'm not in a rush to jump to the hardest thing. I am rather building my fundamentals in C# and C++ properly, shipping small, and then go to making bigger projects: a dream a trilogy of games starting with "The Realm of the Banished" .

RS

Roles I'm after

Open to opportunities across:

Game Design
Level Design
Gameplay Programming
Game Art

Featured projects

Games I've built

01 Godot · Jam

Prisma

A Brackeys Jam parkour-narrative through the phases of life, with Kratos-style voice acting. Ranked 344th overall, 146th in Audio.

GDScript Narrative
Play on itch.io
02 Ongoing · Pt.1

THE REALM OF THE BANISHED

God of War axe mechanic, Portal-style portals, time slowdown, wall-climbing and double jump. Part 1 of a planned trilogy, in active development.

FPS Systems
Follow the devlog on Bluesky
03 Godot · Jam

In the End

Mini Jam : ranked 52nd overall. A top-down shooter: a lone cell versus toxic-wave invaders, with a dash mechanic and reactive enemy AI.

2D Shooter
Play on itch.io
04 Godot · Jam

TaskMaster.exe

A 4th-wall-breaking AI procrastination sim that assigns tasks on your actual desktop. Brackeys Jam, ranked 70th in Innovation.

UI/UX Innovation
Play on itch.io
05 Unreal

FPS Horror Template

An Unreal Engine horror starter: stamina system, sprint, flashlight, and three physics-accurate door types (one-way, two-way, handle-based or push/pull).

Blueprint Horror
Template
06 Godot · Puzzle

Omb

A drag-and-shoot puzzle game, open doors to find treats or bombs. Probability-based outcomes drive a tense risk/reward decision loop.

2D Puzzle
Play on itch.io
07 Godot · IF

Truth or Lie

A detective drag-and-drop narrative, sort evidence as truth or lie as justice and guilt blur. A UI-driven storytelling experiment.

Narrative UI
Play on itch.io
08 Godot · Demo

It Grows

A tense survival demo, survive the cold, escape the unknown. Atmospheric horror built around environmental threat mechanics.

Survival Horror
Play on itch.io

Other projects & experiments

Prototypes, templates & mechanic studies along the way.
Voxel Destruction SandboxGodot

Blast holes in voxel ground, charge-pierce attacks, voxel spheres, and superhero powers with a horde-following enemy algorithm.

Doom-style ShootersGodot

Rotoscoped 2D enemies in 3D space, plus a remixed "Zombat" shooter built on a Brackeys tutorial with my own twist.

Third-Person ControllerGodot

State-based animation management: jump, double jump, roll, fly mode and smooth FoV transitions.

Soccer Stars RemakeGodot

A turn-based football-puck game recreating the flick-and-collide Soccer Stars gameplay loop.

Godot OSOngoing

An experiment building a working operating-system interface entirely inside the Godot engine.

TPS Lock-On TemplateUnreal

Third-person template with target lock-on, strafing, and walk/sprint/crouch blends via Animation Blend Trees.

Chess TemplateUnity

Turn-based chess board with click-to-select pieces and legal-move highlighting.

FPS Camera SystemUnity

A first-person camera and look system, an early study while learning C# in Unity.

Engines & languages

My toolbox

PYTHON, C, C++, C#, ROS, GITHUB, MATLAB, SIMULINK, ARDUINO

GodotPrimary
GDScriptComfortable
Unreal Engine / BlueprintLearning
C++ / C#Building basics

Unreal

VS Code
FL Studio

What I build

Things I love to make

FPS / TPS Controllers

God of War axe throws, portals, wall-run & slide, double jump, head bob, aiming, and fly-mode FoV transitions.

Gameplay Mechanics & AI

Time slowdown, dash, voxel destruction, state machines, plus enemy following algorithms and wave systems.

Narrative & Voice Acting

Story-driven design carried by original, Kratos-style voice work, praised by players as a highlight of Prisma.

Level & Puzzle Design

Parkour flow through the phases of life, and probability-based risk/reward puzzle loops.

Music & Audio

Original scores and soulful in-game audio: the sound and atmosphere players keep calling out.

UI / UX Systems

Drag-and-drop interactions and a 4th-wall-breaking sim that hands you tasks on your real desktop.

Straight from the comment section

What players SAY ABOUT MY GAMES

Read them on itch.io

People's comments from itch.io & the Brackeys Jam community, mostly from Prisma, with a little love for TaskMaster.exe. Replies are mine.

P
PsychoKineticStudios
Prisma

I love your voice and commentary! I love the story (it kept me hooked to see what was next)! I love the glow effect / fog in the scene, and the game feel: running, jumping, sliding!

1
RS Rama replied

Thank you for your kind words, your feedback means a lot!

B
barrett_krebs
TaskMaster.exe

This was a 10/10 game, I'm giving it all five stars in rating!

RS Rama replied

Thank you for the rating and feedback 😁 multi-platform might come in the future. Stay tuned.

H
Hazzy
TaskMaster.exe

Neat idea, a fun concept; the main scare is it re-opening itself. Keen to see where it goes from here.

1
RS Rama replied

Thanks for the feedback! It's still in progress. I've added more detail since, and juggling college with a game jam is tricky 😁

B
Boobjek
Prisma

It's a very cool game! I liked the controls, the atmosphere is as strange as it should be. Very nice voice acting, that deep bass, amazing.

1
RS Rama replied

Thank you for your kind words!!

Z
ZekaZeroTwo
Prisma

Very cool. I like the style and voice. Movement feels pretty good and there are a lot of movement options. Overall nice entry :)

1
RS Rama replied

Thank you very much for your kind words!!

P
Pfoursync
Prisma

Control is very smooth and feels natural. Your game feels right: everything fits together. Congratulations!

1
RS Rama replied

Thank you for playing my game!!

V
VishnyaBU
Prisma

For a solo project, great, really. And the atmosphere is nice.

1
RS Rama replied

Thank you for your kind words!

P
Phanx4lux
Prisma

I really liked your game, especially the atmosphere and sounds.

1
T
torlai
Prisma

Good job on atmosphere. controls are pretty good too. Good work!

1

Max_514
Prisma

Love the visuals, movement feels great to use, well done.

1

yevgenko
Prisma

Nice game, very atmospheric with good sounds and visuals.

1
RS Rama replied

Thank you for your feedback!!

Prisma

Stuck on the third level but this game is very cool! I really like the ambient.

1

Prisma

Nicely made game. Well done!

1
RS Rama replied

Thank you for playing my game and the feedback!!

Prisma

Congrats on making a game! Very well done.

RS Rama replied

Thank you!!

This was a simple but fun experience! I really liked the visuals.

RS Rama replied

role, or just hello?

I'm currently open to roles in game design, level design, gameplay programming, and game art, and always up for collaborating on something fun.
